participants.extracted: Adults and adolescents aged 18 to 65 years with relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (RRMS) meeting McDonald criteria, EDSS score 0 to 5, and at least two clinically documented relapses in the previous 3 years with at least one relapse in the prior 12 months. In the intensive PK/PD subgroup analyzed here, 44 patients consented and 25 patients with sufficient samples were evaluated (4 men and 21 women receiving peginterferon beta-1a; 12 on every-2-week dosing and 13 on every-4-week dosing), with mean age 36.2 years, mean weight 69.9 kg, mean BMI 25.7 kg/m2, and mean BSA 1.78 m2.

interventions.extracted: Peginterferon beta-1a administered subcutaneously at a dose of 125 μg, compared across two dosing schedules: every 2 weeks and every 4 weeks.

outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure clinical and MRI outcomes in relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis over the 1-year placebo-controlled period and through year 2 follow-up. Planned outcomes included annualized relapse rate (ARR) as the primary endpoint; risk of relapse as a secondary endpoint; risk of disability progression as a secondary endpoint, assessed using the Expanded Disability Status Scale (EDSS); the number of new or newly enlarging T2 lesions on MRI as a secondary endpoint; and gadolinium-enhancing (Gd+) lesions on MRI as a tertiary endpoint. The PK/PD substudy also planned to measure peginterferon beta-1a pharmacokinetic parameters from blood sampling, including AUC(0,τ), AUC(0,168 h), Cmax, tmax, CL/F, t1/2, and Vd/F, and pharmacodynamic neopterin parameters including peak neopterin concentration (Epeak), EAUC(0,336 h), and time to maximum effect (Etmax) at intensive sampling time points in week 4 and week 24, with sparse sampling at weeks 4, 12, 24, 56, and 84.
